# Transcrição: 14-ACTIVITY- Ending Your Professional or Business Story

- **Fonte:** 14-ACTIVITY- Ending Your Professional or Business Story
- **Duração:** 01:53
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:53]

Excellent job. I love the way you prepared the ending of that personal story. I can't wait to hear it. Now it's time to do the same for your professional story. For your professional story, there's a couple of considerations you need to take into account. First, in almost all professional stories, you have to show it and also say it. Specificity and clarity is more important in business than it is in art. I can leave my audience open to interpretation when I'm telling a personal story, but when it comes to explaining your product, your service, your business, clarity is essential. So you're going to show it and you're almost certainly going to say it as well. Also with a professional story, leaving a little bit off is oftentimes simple because the story of your product, your service, your company, your people, your team is an ongoing story. You don't build things and then presume it's the last thing we built. In a professional story, whether it's our professional journey, the story of our company, the story of the product, the story of the service, there's always the implication that there's something else coming, that there's a new iteration of this thing, that this thing is going to continue to evolve, that you are going to continue to evolve along your professional journey. So because of this, ending with a little bit of wonder is often a lot easier. All you have to do is imply what's next, indicate what your company is looking towards, signal what the next step you or your company are going to make. That is the wonder that you can create in a professional story. It's oftentimes a lot easier and very necessary. So those two small changes you'll want to be thinking about in your professional story, but otherwise all the same rules apply. So go ahead and do that now.
